summary: PR #2646 adjusted Great Bay stage code and symbol metadata. In grGreatBay_801F499C, it removed temporary s32 max_x locals by folding rand_range(...) directly into HSD_JObjSetTranslateX calls, and introduced a separate Vec3 pos2 in case 2. In symbols.txt, it split the grGb_803E3F6C .data blob into a smaller object plus three string symbols to fix data references for grGreatBay_801F4300. Bot output confirms one new matched code item for grGreatBay_801F499C; the PR body claims grGreatBay_801F4300 matched, but the loaded bot report excerpt does not list it as a new code match.

what_changed: In grGreatBay_801F499C case 2, a new local Vec3 pos2 is declared and passed to lb_8000B1CC and grLib_801C96F8 instead of reusing the outer pos.
In three switch cases of grGreatBay_801F499C, temporary s32 max_x locals holding rand_range(...) results were removed.
The rand_range(...) calls are now folded directly into the second argument of HSD_JObjSetTranslateX as grGb_804D69E0.x0->kame_x + rand_range(...).
In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, grGb_803E3F6C was reduced from size 0x58 to 0x34.
New or corrected string symbols were added at grGb_803E3FA0 size 0x21, grGb_803E3FC4 size 0xD, and grGb_803E3FD4 size 0xB.
smart_moves: The author identified that a seemingly harmless temp local affected MWCC stack/register allocation and removed it by expressing the value directly at the use site.
The author used a distinct local Vec3 for the case-2 position flow instead of reusing an outer Vec3, addressing local lifetime or stack-slot interference.
The PR fixed symbol metadata for adjacent .data/string objects instead of compensating in C source for bad data boundaries.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A temporary local that stores a call result can introduce an extra stack slot or register shuffle; for MWCC matching, try folding the expression directly into the consuming call when the source remains readable.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: temp s32 max_x locals forced an extra stack slot and register shuffle; folding rand_range result into HSD_JObjSetTranslateX fixes codegen
raw_diff: three s32 max_x declarations removed and rand_range calls inlined into HSD_JObjSetTranslateX

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a local variable is reused across a larger scope, introducing a separate block-local variable for a distinct use can improve stack/lifetime shape for matching.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: case 2 needed its own Vec3 pos2 local instead of reusing outer pos
raw_diff: Vec3 pos2 added in case 2 and passed to lb_8000B1CC and grLib_801C96F8

decomp_lessons.lesson: If data references look wrong, inspect symbol boundaries: a single .data blob may need to be split into a smaller object plus separate string symbols rather than handled with source-level workarounds.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: grGb_803E3F6C data blob was actually four separate objects; split fixed data references
raw_diff: grGb_803E3F6C size reduced and grGb_803E3FA0, grGb_803E3FC4, grGb_803E3FD4 string symbols added

The PR body states the temp locals forced an extra stack slot and register shuffle.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that all temp locals should be folded into calls; PR evidence only supports this for the rand_range-to-HSD_JObjSetTranslateX pattern in grGreatBay_801F499C.
Do not record grGreatBay_801F4300 as bot-confirmed newly matched from this slice; the loaded bot report only lists grGreatBay_801F499C.
Do not infer semantic names for grGb_803E3FA0, grGb_803E3FC4, or grGb_803E3FD4; the PR used address-style names and only identifies them as strings.
